Salesforce Note – Payless Auto Repair

Stopped by the shop as a lead visit and met with Nathan and the owner. We discussed the EasyPay program and available financing options. Nathan was already very familiar with EasyPay, as he has used it in the past at a different location.

I reviewed our program, process, and how the financing works for their customers. Both Nathan and the owner showed strong interest in offering the program. I was able to get them signed up on the spot.

Subject: 00UTO00000XZppR2AT
Full Comments: I spoke with Wayne, the manager. I stopped by because we used to do a ton of business with the.Katz Group but since the Switch LTO they have not signed back up with us. We were originally told that they did not want to start doing business with us again until we started collecting the $39 fee. My understanding is that they know that we now collect the fee, but in the interim communication has dropped so we never got back into doing business with them again. Wayne and I had a pretty good relationship so I just wanted to put a bug in his ear to see if he would be willing to speak with upper management about giving us another shot. He said that they are going to be in town at his shop tomorrow and he would be glad to talk to them about using EasyPay again. I gave him a couple of my cards and told him that they could reach out to me or Paul since Elise is on leave at the moment. He told me that they are now using snap and KOALAFI but he would love to use us again.

Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Did some digging on the business and said it's up and down depending on the week, asked about EasyPay and said they have been trying to use EasyPay as much as possible but every time they try to log in it doesn't allow them to log in. Asked them to show me and sure enough, it was routing them to the LBC and I showed him how to clear cookies/cache and said this would of been helpful months back because at different times it allowed us in and then would not allow us in. Apologized and said for any help needed to please ring me directly, said they called into MS and someone was trying to walk them through what to do but didn't have the time so they just hung up and used SNAP or AFF is there other providers. Said I really do appreciate the business and from here did some education and training on the NBC, dropped off pens, pads along with my information as the best POC when needed. Lastly, emailed over website banners to this location.

Subject: 00UTO00000XfaSq2AJ
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Spoke with Jim, who provided insight into the six-month promotion that American First Finance initially offered him before removing it. He noted that many shops lost this promotion shortly after it launched, reverting back to the original promotional period. For this specific AAMCO location to continue using the six-month promotion, American First Finance would charge a 10% merchant fee, and an additional 10% would be rolled into the customer's contract. While the extreme flexibility of their program makes me question the exact accuracy of these terms, this structure seems to be a feasible explanation for how they fund their rebates and extended periods. 

Jim expressed dissatisfaction with this change and is highly likely to convert his business to us if his next few applications successfully close his pending jobs. As a next step, I need to ensure the Amazon incentive is activated for his location.
_label_Full Comments:

Subject: 00UTO00000Xi1bh2AB
Full Comments: :I spoke with the owner, Todd. He told me that Michael no longer is the manager at the shop. He introduced me to Matthew, the newest manager. Matthew is a little quirky and for some reason couldn’t stop talking about how much he liked the competition. He has already brought in a new vendor called BDS and he said that he is trying to get them to offer Acima as well. I tried to get him back on track by asking if he was familiar on how to process EasyPay applications, he said he was but I honestly don’t know if I trust his answer. I also told him that they were on a $20 spiff with us. As I was showing him how to redeem his spiff, he said that it was a waste of time and he doesn’t want it. I honestly don’t think that we will get any sorts of business from them until he leaves. Based on his personality, I’m not sure how long he will be at the shop so hopefully this is short-lived. I left my card and told him to reach out if he needed any assistance.
_label_Full Comments: :I spoke with the owner, Todd. He told me that Michael no longer is the manager at the shop. He introduced me to Matthew, the newest manager. Matthew is a little quirky and for some reason couldn’t stop talking about how much he liked the competition. He has already brought in a new vendor called BDS and he said that he is trying to get them to offer Acima as well. I tried to get him back on track by asking if he was familiar on how to process EasyPay applications, he said he was but I honestly don’t know if I trust his answer. I also told him that they were on a $20 spiff with us. As I was showing him how to redeem his spiff, he said that it was a waste of time and he doesn’t want it. I honestly don’t think that we will get any sorts of business from them until he leaves. Based on his personality, I’m not sure how long he will be at the shop so hopefully this is short-lived. I left my card and told him to reach out if he needed any assistance.

Subject: 00UTO00000XnxaD2AR
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Spoke with Samuel, the shop owner. He mentioned that they are still pushing and offering the financing, but lately customers are not wanting to take the risk. Many people are being more careful with their money and prefer to wait until they have the cash to pay for repairs.

He also mentioned that this seems to be becoming a trend in the Valley. The current ICE situation is also affecting the area. According to Samuel, a couple of factors are impacting business right now—mainly gas prices and people being very cautious with their spending. Because of this, many customers prefer to pay in cash and wait until they have the money available before moving forward with repairs.

Subject: 00UTO00000XqzkL2AR
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
I met with Brayden today regarding the financing situation at his AAMCO locations. He informed me that he recently began partnering with Koalifi, American First, and SNAP. This decision was driven by a high number of customer declines he experienced during the fall when we were operating as his sole financing option. Brayden explicitly stated that he would prefer to use our services, but he needs to see consistently higher approval rates and fewer declines to make that viable. Consequently, this shift to utilizing multiple lenders has resulted in a significantly lower volume of applications coming from his locations, meaning we are currently seeing far less funding overall until we can improve those approval numbers.

Subject: 00UTO00000XwRb72AF
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Spoke with Ken at Midas in Dublin and introduced myself as the Easypay account manager for his area. He appreciated me stopping by and mentioned that usage remains low. He noted that, similar to other stores in Dublin, the credit culture is minimal and most customers pay out-of-pocket. They do have their own corporate credit card, which they usually lead with, but Easypay is presented occasionally when appropriate.

Ken said he would share my information with Junior, the store manager, and that they would reach out if they need anything. This is a very low-usage store, and Dublin overall does not appear to be a high-priority area. Periodic check-ins are sufficient, perhaps once a quarter, with no need for high-touch follow-up.

This aligns with Kenny’s feedback that customers are currently hesitant to invest in vehicle repairs and are trying to delay maintenance where possible.

Subject: 00UTO00000Y6QFc2AN
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Victor,

Thank you for taking the time to chat. Here is a quick overview of EZPay. It’s huge—nationwide, but here in Las Vegas. I currently cover all the Meineke and Midas locations, and we’re seeing great business results.

Here’s how it works:

No cost upfront to the merchant.
We pay every day, Monday through Friday at 1 p.m. (no weekends or holidays).
Jobs completed before 1 p.m. are paid the same day.
Jobs completed after 1 p.m. are paid the next business day.

No recourse – we just ask that you verify the ID to ensure the applicant is the actual customer.
Setting up a business account takes just a few minutes. All we need is the EIN and the account number for deposits. After that, either I or you can fill in the remaining business details.

Once set up, you can immediately call back customers who couldn’t come up with the deductible. You get the job immediately, and the customer gets the service without delay.

Looking forward to helping you get s...

Subject: 00UTO00000YC4SU2A1
_label_Company / Account: Corporate Mattress Brands LLC DBA Mattress Brands Discounters - Conroe
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
I spoke with Brennan regarding the finance option requirements that most major furniture retailers are currently looking for. He emphasized that online transaction capabilities are a top priority to streamline their business, and they will not even consider a partner that cannot offer this feature. Furthermore, they want us to handle the collection of all funds and require a highly automated process to reduce the reliance on salespeople to fill out paperwork, which helps minimize invoice errors. Brennan also noted that some retailers will run multiple financing options to secure the cheapest individual installment payments for their customers. He mentioned that competitors like Snap and Acima are already providing online capabilities similar to what they would expect from our EasyPay product.
_label_Full Comments:

Account/Contact: Eduardo – Garcia’s Tires (Southgate, Imperial & Garfield)
Interaction Type: Store Visit

Summary:
Stopped by Garcia’s Tires in Southgate and spoke with Eduardo regarding current financing practices. The location is associated with Snap; however, financing is not processed directly at this store. Instead, customers are referred to their Long Beach location, where applications are run and completed.

Eduardo shared that they typically utilize Snap about 1–2 times per week. Additionally, the owner operates multiple locations, presenting a broader opportunity for expansion across the business.

This current setup creates inefficiency by sending customers to another location for financing, highlighting an opportunity to introduce EasyPay at the Southgate store to keep transactions in-house and improve conversion.

Eduardo agreed to pass along my business card to the owner for further discussion.

Key Insight...
